channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CSIRO to Cut 92 Environmental Research Roles Amid Organisational Restructure
item.description: Australia’s national science agency, the CSIRO, has confirmed it will cut 92 roles from its environmental research division, reducing the number of research programs from eight to five. The job reduct...

item.title: Municipal rate increases disproportionately impact small and black-owned property businesses in South Africa
item.description: The South African Institute of Black Property Practitioners (SAIBPP) warns that uniform municipal rate increases hit property owners unevenly, disproportionately affecting small and black-owned proper...

item.title: Kelowna man attacked during morning walk, family calls for public safety
item.description: A man visiting from India, Tejinder Singh, was attacked during a morning walk along Highway 97 near Highway 33 in Kelowna, B.C. According to his daughter, Manvir Sandhu, the incident began when a youn...

item.title: England to Offer Free Bus Travel for Children and Suspend Food Tariffs in August
item.description: The UK government has announced a new initiative to support families amid ongoing cost-of-living pressures. Children aged five to fifteen will be able to travel for free on participating local buses a...

item.title: Man dies after alleged arson attack in Pretoria office; suspect arrested
item.description: A 44-year-old man, Tshepo Edward Kabini, tragically died following an alleged workplace attack in Akasia, Pretoria. Authorities report that the suspect, Raymond Mnisi, also 44, allegedly confronted Ka...

item.title: Robert Kearns’ Fight for Recognition Over Intermittent Windshield Wipers
item.description: In the 1960s, engineer Robert Kearns invented the intermittent windshield wiper, inspired by the human eye's blinking mechanism, which allows wipers to pause between swipes. After demonstrating his in...

item.title: Trump outlines underground military and research facilities beneath new White House ballroom
item.description: President Donald Trump has revealed that the new 90,000-square-foot White House ballroom, currently under construction, will include an extensive underground military complex. The project, which has a...

item.title: American Doctor Under Observation in Prague After Ebola Exposure
item.description: An American doctor who was exposed to the Ebola virus has arrived in the Czech Republic and has been admitted to Prague's Bulovka University Hospital for preventive observation. Czech health authoriti...

item.title: Congressional Amendment Proposes Nationwide Restriction on Police License Plate Tracking
item.description: U.S. lawmakers are set to introduce a bipartisan amendment that would prohibit recipients of federal highway funding from using automated license plate readers (ALPRs) for purposes other than toll col...

item.title: Senate leadership discussions continue amid bloc shifts
item.description: Senator Erwin Tulfo confirmed on Thursday that discussions about a potential leadership change at the Philippine Senate are still ongoing. He emphasized that the situation remains fluid, noting the cu...

item.title: Tanzania to Start Liganga and Mchuchuma Mining and Steel Projects Soon
item.description: Tanzania is now ready to kick off the long-awaited Liganga and Mchuchuma integrated projects for iron ore, coal, and steel production within the next three months. The projects, situated in Njombe Reg...

item.title: Blue Jays edge Yankees 2-1 as Yesavage dominates on the mound
item.description: In a highly anticipated matchup between rising stars, Toronto Blue Jays pitcher Trey Yesavage outperformed New York Yankees' Cam Schlittler, leading his team to a 2-1 victory on Wednesday night. The g...

item.title: Putin and Xi Reinforce Strategic Ties with Agreements on Trade, Energy, and Diplomacy
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin met in Beijing shortly after former U.S. President Donald Trump's visit, signalling the strength of their long-standing partnership. B...

item.title: West Bengal Government Initiates Policy to Address Illegal Border Infiltration
item.description: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has announced a new government policy to tackle illegal infiltration in the State. Termed as the “detect, delete and deport” strategy, this policy targets i...

item.title: Chancellor Announces Measures to Reduce Food Prices and Fund Free Bus Travel for Children
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves has unveiled a series of measures aimed at easing the cost of living pressures on UK households, exacerbated by global events such as the Iran conflict. The plan includes cutt...

item.title: APC Chairman Yilwatda Assures Party Can Handle Primaries Disagreements
item.description: The National Chairman of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Nentawe Yilwatda, don talk say the party get capacity to settle any internal wahala wey fit show for primaries. Yilwatda yarn this one aft...

item.title: David Brat Confirmed as US Ambassador to Australia, Focuses on AUKUS and Critical Minerals
item.description: David Brat, the incoming US ambassador to Australia, has been confirmed following a US Senate hearing that highlighted his varied interests and policy priorities. Brat is described as a man of faith, ...

item.title: Colossal Biosciences Develops 3D-Printed Artificial Eggshells to Grow Chicken Embryos
item.description: Colossal Biosciences has announced that it has successfully grown chicken embryos using 3D-printed artificial eggshells. The oval-shaped shells are created with a lattice structure coated internally w...

item.title: Security Forces Kill Four Militants, Including Key Commander, in North Waziristan Operation
item.description: In a significant intelligence-based operation in Spinwam area of North Waziristan, Pakistan's security forces have eliminated four terrorists, including a major militant commander named Umar, also kno...

item.title: Sara Duterte's impeachment proceedings scheduled to start in July
item.description: The impeachment trial of Sara Duterte, former Vice President and daughter of former President Rodrigo Duterte, is expected to begin on July 6, 2026. The House of Representatives has formally endorsed ...
